channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass income and corporate tax cuts, raising statewide sales tax to pay for it
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana’s GOP-dominated legislature passed tax cuts on personal and corporate income on Friday in exchange for a statewide sales tax increase, giving Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News contributor Janette Nesheiwat tapped as Trump's surgeon general
item.description: President-elect Trump named Janette Nesheiwat, a Fox News contributor and medical doctor in New York and New Jersey, as his nominee for surgeon general in his second administration. "I am proud to announce that Dr. Janette Nesheiwat will be the Nation’s Doctor as the United States Surgeon General.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Chavez-DeRemer for Labor secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer (R-Ore.), to serve as Labor secretary in his new administration. Chavez-DeRemer had the backing of the Teamsters before she was officially picked by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident that has the animal's owner fuming, but that local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas man killed by police while on 911 call
item.description: Brandon Durham, 43, of Las Vegas, died at the home on Tuesday, Nov. 12. Police said he was pronounced deceased at a hospital a short time thereafter.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, NewsNation

item.title: Teen suspect arrested after cheerleader found dead in woods
item.description: Tennessee cheerleader Savannah Copeland, 13, was allegedly stabbed to death by a 15-year-old after the pair met up around midnight Oct. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas approves optional Bible-based lessons for public schools
item.description: Texas gave final approval Friday to optional biblical lesson plans for kindergarten through 5th grade classes in state public schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman in coma after trying to save pro golfer's 6-year-old son from drowning
item.description: Professional golfer Brian Harman revealed that a close family friend has been in a coma for weeks after trying to save his 6-year-old son from a near-drowning.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: President of Atlanta's historically Black Spelman College steps down after leave of absence
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Spelman College's president won't be coming back from her leave of absence and is stepping down permanently, the historically Black women's college announced Thursday. The college, which has more than 3,000 students, hasn't said why Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State of the Rio Grande: Officials discuss new sources of water from Mexico
item.description: Texas and Mexican officials held a seminar Thursday to hash out the current and future State of the Rio Grande as waters in the international river continue to dwindle, threatening border communities.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Clonazepam tablets recalled over 'life-threatening' label mistake
item.description: The company that produces the tablets has said the medication is mislabeled with the incorrect strength on the carton.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Perez Hilton says Jussie Smollett decision won't help 'tanked' career: Court didn't dispute hate crime hoax
item.description: Pop culture expert and crime buff Perez Hilton weighs in on the Illinois Supreme Court's decision to toss the hate crime hoax conviction of Jussie Smollett.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No charges filed against man arrested after teens set up sting
item.description: A 46-year-old California man ambushed by vigilante teens after he allegedly provided a girl with nude photos of himself and tried to arrange a sexual encounter with her is, so far, not facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | LIST
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man wins $650K on lottery ticket using numbers tied to late wife
item.description: A Chicago man won a $650,000 Lucky Day Lotto jackpot prize after playing numbers related to his late wife.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KRON4
